title: Parameterize Terraform With Variables
description:
## Make the Config Reusable

**Your task:** write **`/root/main.tf`** that:
1. declares a **`variable "environment"`** with a **`default`** of **`"dev"`**
2. declares a **`local_file`** resource named **`config`** whose **`content`** **references the variable** (uses `var.environment`)

```hcl
variable "environment" {
  default = "dev"
}

resource "local_file" "config" {
  content  = "env=${var.environment}"
  filename = "/tmp/app.env"
}
```

> 💡 No `terraform` binary is needed — this checks that the HCL declares the variable and references it via `var.environment`.

hints.title: Variable + interpolation
hints.body: Declare the variable block with a default, then interpolate it in the resource with var.<name>.

validation.description: Checks for a variable block with a dev default and a resource referencing var.environment.
